name: Toni Preckwinkle
bio: Toni Preckwinkle is the incumbent Cook County Board President, first elected in 2010, and chair of the Cook County Democratic Party. She previously served as a Chicago alderperson representing the 4th Ward for nearly two decades. (Source: Cook County official website; WTTW Voter Guide 2026)
prior_office: Cook County Board President (2010–present); Chicago Alderman (4th Ward, 1991–2010)

jacket.note: County race — ILSBE filing, not FEC.

red_flags.label: Connected to Ald. Burke Federal Corruption Case
red_flags.detail: Preckwinkle returned over $100,000 in fundraising linked to indicted Chicago Alderman Edward Burke following his 2019 federal corruption charges — a documented fact per Chicago Tribune reporting. Reports have also alleged she hired Burke's son as a six-figure county employee, though this has not been independently confirmed. Preckwinkle was not charged in connection with the Burke case and has denied any quid pro quo arrangement.

red_flags.label: Soda Tax Backlash — Revenue Motive Admitted
red_flags.detail: Preckwinkle championed a controversial penny-per-ounce soda tax in 2017, which reached 87% disapproval in polls and was repealed. She later admitted the primary motivation was revenue, not public health.

endorsements.significance: Mayoral endorsement signals progressive coalition alignment

career_history.highlight: Longest-serving County Board President; oversaw $6B+ budget

policy_platform.position: Supported bail reform and reducing pre-trial detention. Has called for accountability at Cook County Jail — complicated by reliance on Sheriff Dart.
Led Cook County Health expansion; championed mental health services as alternative to incarceration.
Passed controversial soda tax (2016, later repealed). Supports progressive revenue tools.

social_pulse.summary: Establishment Democrat with mixed sentiment. Respected for longevity and institutional competence; criticized by progressives for relationships with machine figures like Dart. High name recognition.

trust_indicators.label: Soda tax controversy (2016) — passed then repealed
No personal ethics violations on record
Cook County Democratic Party Chair
trust_indicators.value: Dual role raises questions about conflicts of interest
